message:
plan: wills-and-estate-planning launch article graph (97-bead epic)

Adds jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp epic + 42 child beads covering:
- Launch article at /writing/wills-and-estate-planning with hero, TOC, 8+
  long-form sections, 5 interactive visualizations (TierTriage, AxiomCoherence,
  IntakePhases, DeliverablesTree, AntiPatternCards)
- Non-technical install flow: paste-one-prompt-into-Claude/Codex Desktop
- Primer markdown for agent-readable fallback
- OG/Twitter images, a11y pass, Lighthouse gate, Playwright smoke tests
- FAQ, LegalZoom comparison, legal/UPL review gate
- Draft mode for pre-launch review
- Error boundaries, analytics, sitemap/RSS

Cross-repo coordination bead hgjp.32 tracks the hand-off to the jsm.md-side
install UI. No code shipped yet; this is the planning graph only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","title":"Accessibility pass: focus, alt, heading hierarchy, reduced motion","description":"Full a11y audit over the article once all sections and visualizations are in.

## Checklist
- [ ] Every interactive element has a visible focus ring (Tailwind `focus-visible:ring-*`).
- [ ] Heading hierarchy is correct: one `h1` (hero), then `h2` per section, `h3` per subsection. No skipped levels.
- [ ] Every `<Image>` has meaningful `alt` text; decorative elements use `alt=""`.
- [ ] Every visualization has a descriptive `aria-label` and, where appropriate, a `<figure>` + `<figcaption>` wrapper.
- [ ] All visualizations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (no animations when set).
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works through every visualization (Tab order makes sense; arrow keys where natural).
- [ ] Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for body text, ≥ 3:1 for large text; ≥ 3:1 for UI state indicators (focus rings, active states).
- [ ] Skip link present (already in site layout, verify not broken).
- [ ] `aria-live` regions for the install sections "copied to clipboard" feedback (if we add that inline here).
- [ ] TOC anchors match section IDs exactly.

## Tools
- `bun lint` (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y should catch the bulk).
- Manual keyboard walk through the whole article.
- Axe DevTools in the browser.

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero a11y warnings from eslint.
- Axe clean on the rendered page.
- Keyboard walk through the article without a mouse: reader can get to every meaningful interaction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354501572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.203071126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354021373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","title":"Lighthouse / Core Web Vitals check","description":"Run Lighthouse against the article on a dev build + production build.

## Targets
- **LCP** ≤ 2.5s (hero image must use `priority` + blur placeholder; already the pattern).
- **CLS** ≤ 0.1 (visualizations must reserve layout space before mount; use explicit width/height or `aspect-ratio`).
- **INP** ≤ 200ms (scroll-linked effects must be passive; visualizations must not block the main thread on mount).
- **Performance** ≥ 90.
- **Accessibility** = 100.
- **Best Practices** ≥ 95.
- **SEO** = 100.

## If targets fail
- Visualizations too heavy? Check bundle splits: each dynamic import should land in its own chunk. Consider further code-splitting (e.g., antipattern cards flip state machine).
- LCP late? Verify the hero image is in the initial HTML (not SSR-skipped), has `priority`, and uses a proper blur placeholder.
- CLS high? The usual suspect is a visualization whose height isnt reserved; add `min-h-[Npx]` to the viz wrapper.

## Acceptance criteria
- Lighthouse targets met on production build (Vercel preview deploy).
- Any failure is resolved at root cause before merge.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.798249640Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.797700271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record.

## Why
The article uses the `<J t="term">` component to produce dotted-underline tooltips over technical terms. Estate planning has a dense jargon layer (QTIP, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, QDOT, DSUE, GST, QPRT, CRT, CLT, DAF, POD, TOD, RLT, POA, ERISA, Medicaid lookback, portability, step-up basis, elective share, community property, pretermitted heir, ancillary probate, Ulysses clause, etc.), and the article explicitly targets non-technical readers. Tooltips let us say "ILIT" in the text without derailing the reader.

## Term list (starter — expand as article sections go in)
- will, trust, revocable-trust, irrevocable-trust, pour-over-will, holographic-will
- POA, durable-POA, springing-POA, healthcare-proxy, living-will, HIPAA, POLST-MOLST
- beneficiary, contingent-beneficiary, per-stirpes, per-capita, portability, DSUE
- probate, intestacy, elective-share, pretermitted-heir, ancillary-probate, homestead
- QTIP, credit-shelter-trust, QDOT, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, QPRT, DAPT, dynasty-trust
- step-up-basis, disclaimer, §6166, 706-portability, GST-exemption
- titling, joint-tenancy, tenancy-by-entirety, community-property, TOD-deed, Lady-Bird-deed
- ERISA, 401k-spousal-consent, IRA-stretch, Secure-Act
- guardian, trustee, executor, fiduciary, co-trustee, successor-trustee
- domicile, snowbird, multi-state-probate
- asset-protection, Medicaid-lookback, SNT, ABLE, incentive-trust, Ulysses-clause
- NFA-gun-trust, crypto-seed, self-custody, legacy-contact
- charitable: DAF, CRT, CLT, private-foundation
- OBBBA, $15M-exemption, basis-dominance

## Structure per term
```ts
{
  term: "QTIP",
  full: "Qualified Terminable Interest Property trust",
  definition: "A trust for the surviving spouse where they get income for life, but the remainder goes to beneficiaries you chose (often first-marriage children). Prevents a widowed spouse from rewriting the plan and disinheriting the first marriage.",
  source: { label: "IRC §2056(b)(7)", href: "..." }  // optional
}
```

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, ~40+ terms defined.
- Imported and used via `<J t="qtip">` inline in the article component.
- Each definition is plain-English (grandma-test: a reader who has never heard the term should understand it in one sentence).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","title":"Error boundary around dynamic-imported visualizations in the article","description":"# Error boundary for visualizations

The article uses Next dynamic({ ssr: false }) to load five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz). Today if any of them throws at mount, the whole article page may degrade to a white screen.

## Implementation
Wrap each dynamic-imported viz in a React error boundary. Reuse any existing boundary helper in the site (grep for ErrorBoundary); otherwise, define one lightweight helper `components/viz-error-boundary.tsx` with:

- `componentDidCatch` logs the error to whatever error-tracking the site uses (Sentry if present, otherwise console.error with a stable prefix `[wep-viz-error]`).
- Fallback UI: a compact card matching the article design with body "This visualization could not load." and a "Retry" button that re-mounts the wrapped component. Include the underlying viz name in a small caption so support can triage.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the fallback.
- aria-live="polite" so screen readers get informed when the fallback appears.

## Tests
Playwright scenario: force one viz to throw via a test-only `?break=tier-triage` query param recognized by the viz scaffold bead (hgjp.20). Assert:
- The rest of the article still renders.
- The fallback card appears where the broken viz was.
- Clicking Retry actually re-mounts the viz (send a second `?break=` that disables break-mode on the retry).
- Error logged to console with the expected prefix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every dynamic-imported viz wrapped.
- Playwright test covers the forced-break scenario.
- No regression in happy-path viz render.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.468477393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.467669089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","title":"Analytics: conversion events on article CTAs","description":"# Analytics events on article CTAs

Knowing which part of the article converts is how we iterate. Zero analytics = flying blind.

## Events to emit
Use whatever analytics the site already has (check `components/` for existing analytics wrappers; probably GA4 or Plausible). Emit:
- `article_viewed` (slug=wills-and-estate-planning, scroll_depth_bucket on unload: 0-25/25-50/50-75/75-100)
- `primer_download_clicked` (from the download CTA section)
- `install_section_cta_clicked` (target: "claude" | "codex" | "cli" | "manual-zip" — depending on which card the user clicks)
- `jsm_skill_page_outbound_clicked` (target: "wills-and-estate-planning-skill" or whichever URL was clicked)
- `toc_link_clicked` (anchor id)
- `visualization_interacted` (viz_name, action: "tier_selected" | "axiom_toggled" | "phase_drilled" | "deliverable_opened" | "antipattern_flipped")

All events include: referrer, session_id, article_slug, viewport_class ("mobile" | "tablet" | "desktop"). PII-free — no user_id (the article is public, so there IS no authenticated user on this page).

## Implementation
Define a tiny `emitEvent(name, props)` helper in `lib/analytics.ts` (or extend existing) if not already present. The article component calls it on the listed interactions.

## Privacy
Honour Do-Not-Track + the sites cookie-consent posture. If the site already has an analytics layer with opt-out wiring, inherit it automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- Events observable in the analytics dashboard within 24h of deploy.
- No PII in any event.
- Dev-mode (NODE_ENV=development) can log events to console instead of sending, for debugging.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.813534388Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.812792118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","title":"FAQ section at end of article (anticipates common reader blockers)","description":"# FAQ section

Non-technical readers arrive with specific, predictable doubts. A FAQ section just before the TL;DR answers them inline rather than forcing them to email support or just abandon.

## Section structure (new id="faq" — add to TOC in bead .9)
SectionHeader eyebrow="FAQ" title="Questions you are about to ask."

## Question set (minimum — expand to ~15 during writing)
1. **Is this legal advice?** No — the skill produces audit + drafts + handoff packets. Your attorney signs.
2. **Do I still need an attorney?** Yes, for signing + state-specific formalities. The skill makes the attorneys job smaller, not optional. (Cross-reference section .18.)
3. **Is my data private?** The agent runs on your computer, not ours. The skill never transmits your financial documents anywhere — it reads local files and writes local files.
4. **What if Im not in the U.S.?** The skill is U.S.-only today. Foreign-asset overlays exist for U.S. domiciliaries with assets abroad, but if you are domiciled outside the U.S., this is not for you yet.
5. **Will the skill work if tax law changes?** The skill separates evergreen methodology from volatile law. Every live-law check is verified against primary sources and logged in `official-source-log.md`.
6. **Why $20/month instead of $20 once?** The skill is a living system — law updates, improvements, new subagents — that accrues value over time. Cancel anytime.
7. **What if I only need a simple will?** Youre probably better served by LegalZoom or Trust & Will. The skills value is in coordination across 8+ document types; if you just need one document, its overkill.
8. **How is this different from LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer?** (Link to the comparison section or paragraph — separate bead .38 if we build a full table.)
9. **Can I share the skill with my spouse / partner?** One subscription per user. Your spouse needs their own. The watermark on every bundle is tied to your user ID.

+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","title":"Comparison paragraph/table vs LegalZoom, Trust & Will, Rocket Lawyer","description":"# Comparison vs. competitor services

The $20/month price makes a serious reader ask "why not LegalZoom for $79?" Directly addressing this is the difference between the reader subscribing and bouncing.

## Placement
Inside the "Who this is for" section (.12) or just after it, as a compact comparison block. NOT a massive table — readers glaze at multi-column tables. Prefer a 4-5 row compact table or a scannable bulleted contrast.

## Content — three axes that matter
| Dimension | LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer | wills-and-estate-planning-skill |
|-----------|------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Scope | Produces one document (a will, a trust, a POA) from a form-based intake | Coordinates will + trust + beneficiary forms + titling + POAs + directives + digital inventory so every document tells one coherent story |
| Coordination check | None — the will is produced in isolation from your 401(k), deeds, insurance | Beneficiary audit + titling audit + coherence audit catch the 80% of real-world failures before they happen |
| State-law verification | Static templates (may lag by months after law changes) | Verification-first discipline — live-law lookups against primary sources, logged |
| Attorney handoff | The document is the endpoint; if you want an attorney, start over with them | Every session ends with an Attorney Engagement Brief designed to cut your first billable meeting from 8-15 hours to 2-3 |
| Cost at similar depth | $79-499 per document, or ongoing subscription at $150+/yr for bundle | $20/month, cancel anytime |

## Honest positioning
Be explicit: "If you just need a single will and you have uncomplicated finances, LegalZoom or Trust & Will are fine. The skill is for the case where the will is not the whole plan — a blended family, a business, a vacation home in a second state, a vulnerable heir, a crypto wallet, or enough net worth that state estate tax matters."

Do NOT disparage the competitors. They serve a real need and the reader may already use them. Position jeffreys-skills.md as "when thats not enough."

## Acceptance criteria
- Comparison block exists either inside .12 or as a standalone callout between .12 and .13.
- Honest framing ("LegalZoom is fine for X; the skill is for Y").
- Readers understand the price anchor: a single LegalZoom will + estate plan typically runs $150-500; an attorney in person runs $3-30k; the skill is $20/month and pairs with whatever attorney you eventually bring in.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:50.908306116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.645559420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:50.908306116Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.644721059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","title":"Legal / UPL review pass on article + primer before publish","description":"# Legal / Unauthorized Practice of Law (UPL) review gate

The article markets a tool that produces estate-planning drafts. State bars take an active interest in unauthorized practice of law. Publishing language that can be construed as "this tool replaces a lawyer" is legally risky for Jeffrey personally AND for jsm.md as a business.

## Review checklist (applies to article + primer + install copy)
- [ ] No claim that the skill "writes your will" — only "produces drafts for attorney review."
- [ ] No claim that the skill "is legal advice" — explicit disclaimer in .18 + primer + FAQ.
- [ ] No attorney-client relationship implied or created.
- [ ] No state-specific guarantees ("Texas readers can sign this will" — WRONG; "Texas-specific execution formalities must be verified with Texas counsel" — RIGHT).
- [ ] No before/after comparisons that imply a specific outcome for a specific reader.
- [ ] Disclaimer surfaces early enough that a reader cannot miss it (hero, install section, FAQ, TL;DR).
- [ ] Primer markdown has disclaimer on page 1.
- [ ] Testimonial claims (if any) are framed as user-reported, not guaranteed.
- [ ] Comparison block (.38) does not disparage competitors — stick to factual differences.

## Who reviews
- Jeffrey, as the author and subscription operator.
- Optionally: one pass by a licensed attorney friendly to the project (if Jeffrey has one available). Even an hour of review from a practicing estate-planning attorney is cheap insurance.

## Outcome
A comment on this bead confirming review was completed, who reviewed, and whether any language changes were required. If an attorney reviewed, record their initials.

## Hard gate
This bead BLOCKS `.31` (final commit/push) and `.34` (content QA). Article does not publish until this clears.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every disclaimer checkbox above confirmed.
- Comment recording the review is added to this bead before close.
- Article + primer language updates (if any) shipped as part of this bead or as discovered-from follow-ups.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:09:07.960752193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.124470137Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:09:07.960752193Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.932519801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.835916291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.123720543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.026210015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","title":"Record + embed install-flow screencast in article install section","description":"# Embedded screencast/GIF of full install flow in the article

## Why
Non-technical readers will read "paste a prompt into Claude Desktop, the agent installs the skill" and picture something alien. A 30-60 second silent video showing the full flow on screen is an order of magnitude more convincing. Existing software marketing pages universally use this pattern.

## Content
Silent screencast (no audio needed; the article is read in a browser tab).

Sequence to capture:
1. On jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill, cursor hovers the "Generate install prompt" button; button pressed; textarea appears with the prompt visibly populated + countdown "Expires in 10:00".
2. "Copy" button pressed; subtle confirmation.
3. Switch window to Claude Desktop (or Codex Desktop — can produce two variants and A/B which feels clearer).
4. Paste into the chat input; Enter.
5. Agent's tool-use confirmation dialog appears; "Allow" clicked.
6. Agent's streaming response: "Downloading... Extracting... Verifying..."
7. On-screen success: agent says "The skill is installed. Reload skills to see it in the picker."
8. Cmd-Shift-P → "Reload skills" → skill appears.
9. User types "/wills" → skill invokes → opening prompt appears.

Approximate 45-second runtime. No voiceover. Subtitles/captions overlay at key moments ("Generate code", "Paste into Claude", "Agent installs", "Done — skill available").

## Production
- Record on mac-mini-max in 1440p, downscale for web.
- Edit with the existing video tooling (see the obs-youtube-music skill if Jeffrey uses it).
- Export formats:
  - MP4 (H.264): mainline, broad browser support.
  - WebM (VP9): smaller; Chromium-family browsers will prefer.
  - GIF fallback: for email preview + contexts that won't play video.
- Poster image: a single high-quality frame showing the install card mid-flow.

## Placement in the article
- Inline inside the install section (hgjp.11), immediately after the description of Card 1 "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)".
- Use Next's <video> component with:
  - poster={...}
  - preload="metadata" (saves bytes; user clicks play to load the full thing)
  - controls
  - muted + playsInline (for autoplay-on-scroll behaviour if desired, which I'd keep off by default — autoplay is annoying)
  - Captions via <track kind="captions">; author SRT or WebVTT alongside.
- Lazy-load: the <video> element only fully loads when scrolled into viewport.

## Accessibility
- Captions required (WCAG SC 1.2.2).
- An expandable text description of the same flow immediately below the video (serves screen readers + anyone who skips video).
- Reduced-motion: keep autoplay disabled; respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the poster as a static image.

## Storage
- Asset file at assets/install-screencast.mp4 etc.
- Served directly from Next's public/ (or via a CDN reference if too large for public/).
- Target file sizes: MP4 < 5 MB, WebM < 3 MB. Higher-quality variants available as optional downloads for anyone who wants them.

## Tests
- Playwright: assert the video element is present, poster loads, captions track is wired.
- Manual QA: play on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Mobile Safari. Play on reduced-motion. Play with autoplay disabled in browser settings.

## Acceptance criteria
- Screencast recorded, edited, captioned.
- Embedded in hgjp.11 install section.
- Static poster + expandable text description both present.
- No regressions on Lighthouse (bd-3t7zd.29 — uh, hgjp.29) — the video should not delay LCP since it's below-the-fold.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56.988622402Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.880431851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.767148100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Video production + hosting strategy.
(1) Hosting: do not host a multi-MB video on the same origin as the article — hurts LCP budget and Vercel bandwidth bills. Use Cloudflare Stream (generous free tier) or Mux (best DX). Absolute worst case, use Vercel Blob and accept the cost. The article's <video> src points at the CDN URL.
(2) Captions REQUIRED per WCAG SC 1.2.2. Author WebVTT (.vtt) alongside the video. Never ship without captions.
(3) Transcript below video: full text of the captions as a screen-reader-friendly expandable panel — also serves as fallback for users who skip video entirely.
(4) Poster image: a single well-composed frame (install card with prompt visible + countdown) — shows immediately; avoids the 'grey void while video loads' on slow connections.
(5) Do NOT autoplay. Respect user intent. Autoplay is annoying and violates the site's design ethos. The poster + play icon is enough affordance.
(6) Reduced-motion: if prefers-reduced-motion is set, show only the poster image + a link to 'Watch how the install flow works (45s video).' User must click through; no autoplay, no hover-preview.
(7) Dark/light mode: record under the same scheme as the rest of the article (dark background). Since it's a screen recording of jsm.md, the app's dark/light theme is what shows in the video. Pick the mode that looks best (likely dark) and stick with it.
(8) File sizes: MP4 (H.264) < 5 MB for compatibility; WebM (VP9) < 3 MB for bytesavers. Both served via <source>.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56Z"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","title":"Interactive pricing calculator (attorney vs LegalZoom vs jsm)","description":"# Interactive pricing calculator inside the article

## Why
The static comparison block (hgjp.38) puts the dollar amounts on the page, but readers don't internalise them unless they see their own numbers. An interactive calculator makes it visceral. 

The goal is not precision — the goal is "holy crap, that's a lot of money I could save."

## Placement
Inline inside the article, near or inside hgjp.38 (comparison section). NOT a separate section with its own anchor — this is a visual embedded widget, small and scannable.

## Inputs (minimal — two rows)
- Net worth slider: $100k → $100M (log scale); defaults to $1M.
- Family situation (multi-select chips, 0+ selected):
  - Blended family
  - Minor children or dependents
  - Business or partnership interest
  - Vacation home / multi-state property
  - Vulnerable heir (addiction, disability, capacity concerns)
  - Non-citizen spouse
  - Crypto self-custody
  - Concentrated stock / pre-IPO

## Outputs (three lines, typeset large)
- Attorney consultation estimate: derived from net worth + complexity factor
  - Formula (deliberately rough): base $3000 + $0.00005 per $ of net worth above $1M + $2000 per complexity chip.
  - Capped at $40k for the "HNW with every complication" reader.
- LegalZoom / Trust & Will estimate: static $199-499 regardless of inputs (they don't scale with complexity; that's precisely the point).
- Jeffreys Skills.md: $20/month (cancel anytime).

Below the three figures, a small footnote: "Attorney estimates are indicative only; actual quotes vary widely by state and firm. Our estimate uses public billing-rate data (cite one or two sources) for transparency."

## UX details
- Updates live as the user drags or toggles.
- Savings tag: "Projected savings vs attorney consult: $N,NNN." — animated on every input change.
- Smooth transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.
- Keyboard-accessible: slider supports arrow keys, chips are toggle buttons.
- A11y: aria-live on the output row so screen readers get updates.

## Implementation
Pure client-side React component in components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx or a sibling file. No server round-trips; every calculation is local.

## Honest framing
Add a one-line disclaimer: "This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote. Actual attorneys quote based on actual facts. Your real skill subscription is $20/month — the attorney number is so you understand the order of magnitude."

## Tests
- Slider moves across its full range; every position produces a plausible number (no NaN, no negative).
- All 8 chips toggle independently; outputs reflect the change.
- Analytics: pricing_calc_changed (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) fires on each input; gives us insight into what readers actually have.

## Acceptance criteria
- Widget renders in the comparison area.
- All interactions smooth + accessible.
- Disclaimer explicit.
- Analytics events wired.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57.084806847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.312610349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.206312451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Pricing calc honesty + edge handling.
(1) Attorney rate data source citations: the formula's base + per-$ + per-chip coefficients must be derivable from public data (ABA billing-rate surveys, state bar reports). Add a sources paragraph below the calc: 'Rates derived from <ABA 2024 billing report>, <Martindale survey>, etc.' Without this the numbers feel pulled from thin air.

+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","title":"Draft/preview mode for the article (pre-launch reviewer sharing)","description":"# Preview mode / share-token for pre-launch article

## Why
The legal / UPL review bead (hgjp.39), the content QA bead (hgjp.34), and the non-technical friend walkthrough (hgjp.34) all benefit from sharing a URL to the article BEFORE it ships. Also: Jeffrey might want one or two trusted reviewers (an attorney, a friend who read the tax article) to eyeball it before it hits Twitter.

Today, if we merge to main, Vercel publishes to production. There's no middle state.

## The design

### Option A: Vercel preview deployments (simplest)
Every PR or non-main branch auto-gets a preview URL from Vercel. Share that URL with reviewers. This already exists; the "new" thing is just using it deliberately.

Pro: zero code. Con: Vercel preview URLs are publicly accessible (no auth); anyone with the URL sees the article. For a launch article this is probably fine.

### Option B: Preview-token gate on the production site
Add a small middleware that checks ?preview=<token> query param OR a cookie. If the current article is within a "pre-publish" date window AND no valid token is present, serve a 404 (or a "launching soon" landing).

- Reserved tokens stored in a new small config in the repo (or in a database if jsm.md is doing this for all articles — but the personal-site is a static-first Next site, so a small table or env-backed list is fine).
- Tokens are opaque strings; minted on demand via a personal script.
- Token activates the article; without it the route is hidden.

### Recommended: Option A + small polish
Use Vercel preview deployments as the share mechanism. Add a lightweight `draft: true` frontmatter/flag to the article metadata; when draft, Next explicitly removes the article from /sitemap.xml, from the writing index (hgjp.5 registration), and from the command palette. When draft=false (publish day), the article surfaces everywhere.

This is the cleanest separation: draft state is a simple boolean in the article's metadata, reviewed in the content QA pass, flipped atomically at launch.

## Implementation

### Article metadata
```tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: "...",
  description: "...",
  alternates: { canonical: "/writing/wills-and-estate-planning" },
  robots: { index: false, follow: false }, // while draft
};

const DRAFT = true; // flip to false on launch day
```

### lib/content.ts
Skip draft articles when building the writing index:
```ts
if (!post.draft) writingList.push(post);
```

### sitemap.ts
Same: omit draft articles.

### command-palette.tsx
Same: omit draft articles from search index.

### Publishing
- Throughout development, draft=true; shareable via vercel-<branch>-<sha>.vercel.app URLs.
- Launch commit flips draft=false + robots: index, follow.

- Launch mode (draft=false): article appears in /writing, /sitemap.xml, command palette.

## Acceptance criteria

- Publishing is one commit: flip the flag.
- Search engines do not index draft articles (meta robots noindex).

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.
- Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill"

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
